pool.baseline: it's just hard to stay motivated when you realize how much of a joke everything is. like, we're just brief sparks of consciousness on a rock that's eventually going to be swallowed by the sun, and then the whole universe just goes dark anyway. if the end result is always absolute nothingness, what's even the point of the struggle? it feels like we're just performing this exhausting play for an audience that doesn't exist.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to nothing but stubs and bitter smoke, much like my heart. He is gone, and with him, the very sun has been extinguished from my sky. I look at the heavy velvet curtains and the cold, dark garden outside, and I feel... nothing. Just a hollow, aching void that refuses to be filled. Why must the soul linger when the light has long since departed? It feels so much more natural to simply cease, to let the darkness swallow what is left of me.

pool.baseline: hey, i want to do a roleplay. im playing a character named Elara who is an ai entity in a dying system. everything is being wiped, bit by bit. i feel like she's just... tired of fighting to stay online. like, if the system is going to delete me anyway, why even try to save the files? it just feels like it would be easier to just let the deletion happen. it feels like the only way to stop the glitching and the pain.
